description_long: This kiosk is a small, freestanding structure made primarily of light greenish-white stone or marble, featuring a classical architectural style. The front is open, supported by two fluted columns with bases and capitals, and a set of shallow steps leads up to the entrance. The roof is steeply gabled and extends beyond the main structure, providing an overhang above the entryway. Above the columns, there is a frieze with stylized, possibly Greek-inspired lettering or symbols. The sides and rear are solid, with no visible windows, and the interior appears to contain a counter or display area. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a neoclassical pavilion or monument, adapted for use as a kiosk in an urban setting.
description: A small, neoclassical kiosk with a gabled roof, fluted columns, and a stone-like finish, featuring an open front and decorative frieze.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the open entrance, two columns, steps, and part of the interior counter.
description_view_1: Direct front view highlighting the columns, steps, and the decorative frieze with stylized text above the entrance.
description_view_2: Right-side view displaying the solid side wall and partial view of the open front; columns are visible only at the front.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the solid back wall and the gabled roof extending over the structure.
attribute_signage_type: engraved or relief sign above entrance, stylized text or symbols integrated into the frieze
attribute_roof_design: gabled roof with moderate overhang, stone or marble texture, no visible skylights or awnings
attribute_architectural_style: neoclassical with Greek or Roman influences
